title: Data Injection via Untrusted Input
description: If the system accepts user input without proper validation and sanitization, an attacker could inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection, XSS) into the system. This could lead to data corruption, unauthorized access, or the execution of arbitrary code on the server.
affected_component: All Input Handling Components (e.g., web application, API)
attack_vector: Unvalidated user input, lack of input sanitization, improper escaping of characters.
mitigations: Implement robust input validation and sanitization
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection
Employ Content Security Policy (CSP) to mitigate XSS attacks
